urgency.message: 6 new CISA KEV addition(s) this week. Patch validation recommended.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 11th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9968, (Thu, Jun 11th)
Chinese, N. Korean Threat Groups Build on Asia-Pacific Success
items.summary: North Korea's gross domestic product (GDP) has grown, in part because of the cybercrime gains of groups linked to the nation, which target business and financial firms.

items.title: Path traversal flaw in AI dev platform Langflow exploited in attacks
items.summary: Attackers are actively exploiting CVE-2026-5027, a high-severity path traversal vulnerability in the AI development platform Langflow, to write arbitrary files on exposed servers. [...]

items.title: CISA Rewrites Federal Patching Requirements for AI Threat Era
items.summary: The new directive gives federal agencies three days to fix the most dangerous flaws, while less severe issues can be deferred.

items.title: CISA Tells US Agencies to Fix Security Bugs in as Little as 3 Days Thanks to AI Threats
items.summary: “Defenders cannot afford to take weeks to patch,” one Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency official warned on Wednesday.

items.title: Trump Risks Key Surveillance Authority Over ‘Unqualified’ Spy-Chief Pick
items.summary: US lawmakers are alarmed that Bill Pulte, a housing official with no intelligence experience, is poised to take charge of one of the government's most powerful surveillance tools.

items.title: The ‘Miasma’ worm source code briefly leaked on GitHub
items.summary: The Miasma credential-stealing attack framework, which has recently targeted open-source ecosystems through supply-chain attacks, was briefly open-sourced on GitHub. [...]

items.title: Bug Bounty Research Triggers ServiceNow Security Alert
items.summary: Bug bounty research inadvertently led organizations to believe they were being breached through their ServiceNow instances.

items.title: CISA to require federal agencies to patch some cyber vulnerabilities within 3 days
items.summary: CISA is giving agencies 180 days to adopt the new patching time frame, according to a directive released Wednesday.

items.title: GitHub announces npm security changes to tackle supply-chain attacks
items.summary: GitHub has announced that npm v12, expected next month, will introduce several security-focused changes aimed at blocking supply-chain attacks abusing behaviors triggered by the 'npm install' command. [...]

items.title: How Federal Agencies Can Activate a Risk Operations Center (ROC) to Meet CISA BOD 26-04
items.summary: Executive Summary Recognizing the ability of Frontier AI models to discover and exploit vulnerabilities at unprecedented speed and scale, CISA’s Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04 marks a significant shift in federal vulnerability management. Rather than prioritizing remediation based o

items.title: AI Risk Worries Insurers and Businesses Alike
items.summary: As companies adopt AI, many insurance firms are explicitly excluding AI risks, while others are forging ahead to create the right framework. What risks can firms reasonably manage?

items.title: Oracle PeopleSoft servers hacked in ShinyHunters data theft attacks
items.summary: Oracle PeopleSoft servers are being targeted in ongoing data theft attacks by the ShinyHunters extortion gang, which claims to have stolen data from over 100 organizations. [...]

items.title: Nightmare-Eclipse Drops Yet Another Microsoft Exploit, RoguePlanet
items.summary: The disgruntled researcher released yet another PoC for a Windows Defender bug that allows for system takeover, showing no signs of abandoning their ongoing feud with Microsoft.

items.title: China-Linked JDY Botnet Expands to 1,500+ Devices for Cyber Reconnaissance
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have warned of a "resurgence and expansion" of JDY, a covert network associated with China-nexus state-sponsored threat actors.

"The JDY botnet comprises over 1,500 SOHO [small office and home office] and IoT devices and operates as a centrally controlled, high-performance

items.title: Fake Software Tutorials on TikTok Spread Vidar Stealer
items.summary: Threat actors push fake free-software tutorials on TikTok and Instagram to spread Vidar stealer

items.title: New SilabRAT Trojan Hijacks Sessions to Steal Crypto
items.summary: MaaS trojan SilabRAT uses HVNC and browser cloning to hijack sessions and steal crypto

items.title: Cybersecurity Software Fails to Detect Fifth of Brower-Based Phishing Attacks
items.summary: Menlo Security research warns that as enterprise applications become increasingly browser based, traditional cybersecurity tools leave them vulnerable to cyber threats

items.title: Cyberattack shuts down major Australian sugar mills, disrupting harvest
items.summary: Australia's second-largest sugar producer said on Wednesday that it was responding to a cybersecurity incident affecting parts of its operations and had engaged cybersecurity experts and local authorities to investigate the attack and restore its systems safely.

items.title: Ivanti, Fortinet, and SAP Release Patches for Multiple Critical Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Fortinet, Ivanti, and SAP have released security updates to address multiple critical security vulnerabilities that could result in arbitrary code execution and information disclosure.

The security flaw patched by Fortinet relates to a command injection vulnerability in FortiSandbox, FortiSandbox C

items.title: Unpatched Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-5027 Exploited for Unauthenticated RCE
items.summary:
A high-severity unpatched security flaw in Langflow, an open-source low-code platform to build artificial intelligence (AI) applications, has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to findings from VulnCheck.

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-5027 (CVSS score: 8.8), a case of

items.title: Turning Millions of Risks Into One Actionable List
items.summary: Every security leader walks into Monday morning with the same question. The findings are there. The dashboards are running. But out of the thousands of critical vulnerabilities on that list, which ones can an attacker actually use against this organization today? Not in theory. Not in a lab. In prod

items.title: China-linked JDY botnet expands targeting of U.S. military networks
items.summary: The JDY botnet, a malware network previously associated with Chinese threat actors like Volt Typhoon, has significantly expanded its targeting scope and reconnaissance efforts. [...]

items.summary:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Tuesday added three new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, following reports of active exploitation.

The list of vulnerabilities is as follows -


  CVE-2026-20245 (CVSS score: 7.8) - An improper

items.title: The 5 Best Practices for Secure Identity Verification
items.summary: Attackers are increasingly bypassing weak authentication through phishing, MFA fatigue, and service desk social engineering. Specops Software breaks down five best practices for stronger identity verification and access security. [...]

items.summary: A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. items.title: Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines
items.summary:
As attackers increasingly favor stolen credentials over exploits, infostealers have become a primary source of access for ransomware and other cybercrime operations.
The post Infostealers Turn Millions of Devices Into Credential Theft Machines appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Wrongful Arrest Exposes Failures in One of the Oldest Police Face-Recognition Tools in the US
items.summary: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.

items.title: Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation
items.summary:
Cyera is positioned as one of the most valuable privately held cybersecurity firms in the world with total funding topping $2 billion.
The post Cyera Raises $600 Million at $12 Billion Valuation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Microsoft patches Exchange Server zero-day exploited in attacks
items.summary: Microsoft has patched an actively exploited Exchange Server vulnerability that allows threat actors to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks targeting Outlook Web Access users. [...]

items.title: Microsoft ships largest Patch Tuesday on record, with one bug under active attack
items.summary: The release comes after Microsoft’s security leadership acknowledged last month that AI tools are driving a surge in vulnerability discovery across the industry.

items.title: Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding
items.summary:
In the post-Mythos era, the company’s platform helps organizations enforce security controls across environments.
The post Aryon Security Raises $29 Million in Series A Funding appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers
items.summary:
Claroty researchers have analyzed the security of Vertiv UPS network cards and the Trane Tracer SC+ HVAC controller.
The post Critical HVAC and UPS Vulnerabilities Could Let Hackers Disrupt Data Centers appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review
items.summary:
Learn more about protecting against unmonitored use of generative AI (Shadow AI) in business units and building and enforcing AI governance frameworks.
The post CISO Forum Webinar Today: 2026 Mid-Year Review appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New Windows Zero-Day Exploit ‘RoguePlanet’ Released
items.summary:
Exploiting a race condition in Microsoft Defender, the exploit leads to local privilege escalation to SYSTEM.

items.title: Microsoft: Some Windows PCs fail to install latest monthly updates
items.summary: Microsoft warned customers on Tuesday that they may have issues installing the latest monthly updates on some Windows devices that were upgraded to Windows 11 24H2 or 25H2. [...]

items.title: After AI Reaches Production: 12 Ways Security Teams Can Take Control
items.summary:
Security teams need more than visibility into AI applications, they need a repeatable framework for monitoring, investigating, and defending them in production.

items.summary: Anthropic unveils Claude Mythos 5 and Fable 5, a restricted-access frontier AI model and guardrailed version for everyone to use

items.title: Your Automated Pentest Looks Clean. See What It Missed in This Expert Webinar
items.summary:

items.title: Over a Quarter of Identity Crime Victims Hit by Multiple Incidents, ITRC Data Shows
items.summary: Nearly 26% of identity crime victims faced multiple incidents in the past year, as ITRC warns of a growing "multi-layered crisis"

items.title: Soccer Fans, You’re Being Watched
items.summary: From anti-drone tech to face recognition, 2026 World Cup stadiums in the US, Canada, and Mexico are subjecting fans to an array of surveillance tech. Here’s what you need to know.

items.title: Mapping Every Flock License Plate Reader Near US World Cup Stadiums
items.summary: Most US World Cup stadiums are surrounded by surveillance cameras. Want to know if you’re being watched on your way to a match? These maps will help you.
